tier_recommendation.description: Three-tier NAS plan for OFDMDetector. Epochs are controllable via CLI --epochs flag. The latency budget is extremely tight (0.05 ms target vs 0.0362 ms baseline = 0.0138 ms headroom). The search space should favor models near or below baseline size.

proposed_tiers.label: Screening
proposed_tiers.description: Fast screening tier — 1 epoch per candidate (~2.2s). Use for early population evaluation and pruning. Identifies architectures that fit within latency budget while showing initial learning signal.

proposed_tiers.label: Development
proposed_tiers.description: Intermediate tier — 5 epochs per candidate (~11s), matching baseline training duration. Used for medium-fidelity evaluation of promising architectures from Tier 1.

proposed_tiers.label: Refinement
proposed_tiers.description: Full refinement tier — 15 epochs per candidate (~33s). Extended training for top candidates to obtain best possible accuracy before final selection. Only the most promising few architectures should reach this tier.

reasoning.notes: Since epochs are controllable via --epochs CLI flag, we propose 3 tiers with increasing epoch budgets. Tier 1 (1 epoch) for fast screening, Tier 2 (5 epochs) matching baseline, Tier 3 (15 epochs) for thorough final evaluation. The tight latency constraint (0.05 ms) means the NAS should favor compact architectures. The baseline model at 0.0362 ms has only 38% headroom — searching for architectures significantly larger than baseline will likely exceed the latency target.
